Carbon fixation

Author

george.westmeijer@umu.se

Published

May 7, 2026

Read input files

The file ‘c-fixation-kos.gtdb-hits.tsv.gz’ contains the genomes’ accessions that have one or more hits of KOs part of a carbon fixation module.

Expand code 
hits <- read_tsv('data/c-fixation-kos.gtdb-hits.tsv.gz', col_types = 'ccccdddc') %>%
  select(-sign, -thrshld) %>%
  #filter(evalue < 1e-6) %>% # This threshold is arbitrary for now
  rename(accession = genome_accno)
Expand code 
gtdb <- read_tsv('../gtdb/ar53_metadata_r232.tsv.gz', show_col_types = F) %>%
  select(accession, ncbi_genbank_assembly_accession, taxonomy = gtdb_taxonomy) %>%
  mutate(accession = gsub('^.._', '', accession)) %>%
  mutate(taxonomy = gsub('.__', '', taxonomy))

gtdb <- read_tsv('../gtdb/bac120_metadata_r232.tsv.gz', show_col_types = F) %>%
  select(accession, ncbi_genbank_assembly_accession, taxonomy = gtdb_taxonomy) %>%
  mutate(accession = gsub('^.._', '', accession)) %>%
  mutate(taxonomy = gsub('.__', '', taxonomy)) %>%
  rbind(gtdb) %>% 
  pivot_longer(1:2, values_to = 'accession') %>% 
  select(accession, taxonomy) %>% 
  separate(taxonomy, into = c('domain', 'phylum', 'class', 'order', 'family', 'genus', 'species'), sep = ';') %>% 
  distinct()

KO matrix

The mclink function is used to calculate the completeness of the six relevant modules (M00165, M00377, M00173, M00376, M00375, M00374). The presence of any KO for each step indicates completeness, and the completeness is a score ranging from 0 to 1. The average value of this score is the genome-specific module completeness score. For example, a score of 0.9 for a module containing 10 steps (reactions) implies 9/10 genes were present.

Expand code 
kos <- hits %>% 
  select(ko, accession) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(presence = 1) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = 'accession', values_from = 'presence', values_fill = 0) 

# This is the actual analysis
mcl <- mclink(data = data.frame(kos))
[2026-05-07 13:45:53] mclink started!
[2026-05-07 13:45:53] Input Sample-KO table type: completeness
[2026-05-07 13:45:53] Scale method for plus: mean
[2026-05-07 13:45:53] Scale method for comma: max
[2026-05-07 13:45:53] Pathway information dataframe successfully imported.
[2026-05-07 13:45:53] There are 494 Modules in the Pathway information dataframe.
[2026-05-07 13:45:53] Genome-KO File successfully imported.
[2026-05-07 13:45:53] There are 100 intersect KOs between the KO list and input dataframe.
[2026-05-07 13:45:53] Converting abundance table to completeness table...
[2026-05-07 13:46:23] Summarizing present KOs of respective modules...
[2026-05-07 13:46:28] mclink finished!
Expand code 
# This extracts the data
cov <- mcl[['coverage']] %>% 
  as_tibble() %>% 
  rename(module = Module_Entry, name = Module_Name) %>%
  select(-Level_2, -Level_3, -Definition) %>% 
  filter(module %in% c('M00165','M00377','M00173','M00376','M00375','M00374')) %>% 
  pivot_longer(3:ncol(.), names_to = 'accession', values_to = 'score')

Collapse archaeal phylogeny on phylum level

Expand code 
# Read the tree (archaeal) tree and remove the 'GB' or 'RS' prefix
tree <- ape::read.tree('../gtdb/ar53_r232.nwk')
tree$tip.label <- gsub('^.._', '', tree$tip.label)

# getMRCA() only works when two or more tips are provided. So remove clades with a single representative

i <- gtdb %>%
  filter(domain == 'Archaea') %>% 
  select(accession, phylum) %>% 
  # Consider only genomes represented in the tree
  filter(accession %in% tree$tip.label) %>%
  tibble::deframe()

# Assigns the accessions of each phyla in a list
groups <- split(names(i), i)

# Compute one representative node per phylum
phy_nodes <- sapply(groups, function(tips) {
  if (length(tips) > 1) {
    ape::getMRCA(tree, tips) # If the clade has more than one genome, find the MRCA
  } else {
    which(tree$tip.label == tips) # If only one tip, return its position in the tree
  }
})
Expand code 
# Ensure node indices are numeric and named by phylum (if all is well, this shouldn't change the data)
phy_nodes <- as.numeric(phy_nodes)
names(phy_nodes) <- names(groups)

#node <- phy_nodes[[fam]]
#tree$node.label <- rep(NA, tree$Nnode) # Remove node labels and replace with NA

# Indices for tip labels
tip_idx <- seq_along(tree$tip.label)

# Assign phylum names either to tips or internal nodes
for (p in names(phy_nodes)) {
  node <- phy_nodes[[p]]
  
  if (node <= length(tree$tip.label)) {
    # If node index corresponds to a tip - rename the tip
    tree$tip.label[node] <- p
  } else {
    # Otherwise it's an internal node → assign node label
    tree$node.label[node - length(tree$tip.label)] <- p
  }
}
Expand code 
arctree <- ggtree(tree) # This reads the original phylogeny into a ggtree object

# Collapse clades (multi-tip phyla) into single branches
for (p in names(groups)) {
  tips <- groups[[p]]
  
  if (length(tips) > 1) {
    node <- phy_nodes[p]
    if (!is.null(node)) {
      arctree <- collapse(arctree, node = node)
    }
  }
}
Expand code 
for (p in names(phy_nodes)) {
  node <- phy_nodes[p]
  
  if (!is.null(node)) {
    arctree <- arctree + geom_hilight(node = node, alpha = 0.3)
  }
}
Expand code 
arctree +
  geom_tiplab(size = 2) +
  geom_nodelab(aes(label = label), size = 2, hjust = 0)
Expand code 
ggsave(filename = 'figures/gtdb_r232_arc_phylum.png', width = 180, height = 240, units = 'mm')

Bacterial phylogeny - phylum

Expand code 
# Read the tree (bacterial) tree and remove the 'GB' or 'RS' prefix
tree <- ape::read.tree('../gtdb/bac120_r232.tree')
tree$tip.label <- gsub('^.._', '', tree$tip.label)

# getMRCA() only works when two or more tips are provided. So remove clades with a single representative

i <- gtdb %>%
  filter(domain == 'Bacteria') %>% 
  select(accession, phylum) %>% 
  # Consider only genomes represented in the tree
  filter(accession %in% tree$tip.label) %>%
  tibble::deframe()

# Assigns the accessions of each phyla in a list
groups <- split(names(i), i)

# Compute one representative node per phylum
phy_nodes <- sapply(groups, function(tips) {
  if (length(tips) > 1) {
    ape::getMRCA(tree, tips) # If the clade has more than one genome, find the MRCA
  } else {
    which(tree$tip.label == tips) # If only one tip, return its position in the tree
  }
})
Expand code 
# Ensure node indices are numeric and named by phylum (if all is well, this shouldn't change the data)
phy_nodes <- as.numeric(phy_nodes)
names(phy_nodes) <- names(groups)

#node <- phy_nodes[[fam]]
#tree$node.label <- rep(NA, tree$Nnode) # Remove node labels and replace with NA

# Indices for tip labels
tip_idx <- seq_along(tree$tip.label)

# Assign phylum names either to tips or internal nodes
for (p in names(phy_nodes)) {
  node <- phy_nodes[[p]]
  
  if (node <= length(tree$tip.label)) {
    # If node index corresponds to a tip - rename the tip
    tree$tip.label[node] <- p
  } else {
    # Otherwise it's an internal node → assign node label
    tree$node.label[node - length(tree$tip.label)] <- p
  }
}
Expand code 
# Plot and collapse ('ctree' for 'collapsed tree')
bactree <- ggtree(tree)

# Collapse clades (multi-tip phyla) into single branches
for (p in names(groups)) {
  tips <- groups[[p]]
  
  if (length(tips) > 1) {
    node <- phy_nodes[p]
    if (!is.null(node)) {
      bactree <- collapse(bactree, node = node)
    }
  }
}
Expand code 
bactree +
  geom_tiplab(size = 2) +
  geom_nodelab(aes(label = label), size = 2, hjust = 0)
Expand code 
ggsave('figures/gtdb-r232-phylum-bac.png', height = 240, width = 180, units = 'mm')
